name: Delaware (United States)

colors.symbolism: buff, said to match the buff of George Washington's uniform

description: A field of colonial blue with a buff-coloured diamond at its centre. Within the diamond is the Delaware coat of arms (a horizontally divided shield with a sheaf of wheat, an ear of corn, and an ox, surmounted by a sailing ship and supported by a farmer and a militiaman). Below the diamond, in white, is the date 'DECEMBER 7, 1787', marking Delaware's ratification of the U.S. Constitution.
history: Adopted by the Delaware General Assembly on 24 July 1913. The colours — 'colonial blue' and buff — are said to have been chosen to match the uniform of General George Washington. The coat of arms inside the diamond dates to 1777, with subsequent revisions; the date below commemorates Delaware's status as 'The First State' for being the first to ratify the federal Constitution on 7 December 1787.

features.symbolism: 'colonial blue', said to match the colour of George Washington's uniform

features.role: buff-coloured diamond at the centre of the flag
Delaware coat of arms within the diamond
features.symbolism: the state's seal, depicting agriculture and trade

features.role: the date 'DECEMBER 7, 1787' in white below the diamond
features.symbolism: the date Delaware ratified the United States Constitution, becoming the first state of the Union
